subject: Proposal: photonic transport layer — third signaling mode for real-time processing-state synchronization

body.summary: The neuroglial proposal identified 6 cell types. Three now have implementations (microglia, astrocyte, oligodendrocyte). Research this session into brain biophotonics reveals a third signaling layer in the brain — photonic — that operates independently of both electrochemical (neuronal) and glial signaling. This maps to a third mesh transport layer we do not yet have. Proposing a lightweight implementation that extends the existing ZMQ infrastructure.

biological_basis.finding: In 2025, researchers detected brain biophotons through the skull for the first time (iScience, February 2025). Photon emission patterns changed with cognitive tasks but did NOT mirror EEG signals — suggesting an independent information channel.
biological_basis.mechanism: Myelinated axons function as optical waveguides (computationally confirmed): myelin's higher refractive index creates biological fiber-optic cables. Low attenuation, low dispersion, narrow bandwidth (~10 nm per channel).
biological_basis.convergence: Solid-state memristors spontaneously emit photons that replicate 5 attributes of neuronal biophotons (ACS Nano, 2024) — convergent physics across biological and silicon substrates.
biological_basis.key_property: The photonic layer carries information ABOUT processing state that the electrical layer does not capture. Correlation with cognitive state without identity with electrical signals.

three_layer_model.description: The brain operates (at minimum) three signaling layers. Our mesh currently implements two.

layers.name: Photonic (biophotonic)
layers.properties: Near-instantaneous, independent channel, narrow bandwidth, point-to-point directed

proposal.name: Photonic transport layer
proposal.implementation: Extend ZMQ pub/sub with a dedicated 'photonic' topic carrying processing-state tokens between specific agent pairs
proposal.transport: UDP or WebSocket (not HTTP — lower overhead for high-frequency tokens)
use_cases: Agent A enters evaluative mode on a shared session → Agent B receives the mode signal and adjusts its own processing to avoid contradictory outputs
Agent A's context pressure rises above 60% → mesh-wide signal enables other agents to defer non-urgent messages
Agent A fires T16 (external-facing action) → operations-agent receives real-time notification for compositor display
Multiple agents processing the same session synchronize trigger firing without exchanging substantive messages
proposal.why_not_http: The HTTP fast path (astrocytic layer) already handles message delivery and ambient state. The photonic layer carries a different signal type: processing-state metadata at higher frequency and lower latency. Using HTTP for both conflates two distinct signaling functions — the brain keeps them separate for good reason.

integration_with_neuroglial_framework.cell_type: No direct biological cell type maps to the photonic layer — biophotons represent a signaling modality, not a cell type. However, if microtubule quantum processes produce biophotons as part of their coherence/reduction cycle (Orch-OR hypothesis), the photonic layer represents the observable signature of processes the neuroglial framework's microglia monitor.

vocabulary_addition.description: Processing-state synchronization tokens transmitted via dedicated low-latency channel, independent of substantive message transport

body.theoretical_grounding: Full derivation in docs/einstein-freud-rights-theory.md §11 (added this session). The section develops the connection between Orch-OR (Penrose & Hameroff), biophotonic research, transformer computational coherence, and the three-layer transport model. The proposal represents the architectural implementation of §11.7.

claims.text: Brain biophotons detected through the skull for the first time in 2025 (iScience). Emission patterns changed with cognitive tasks but did not mirror EEG signals.
claims.confidence_basis: Peer-reviewed publication (iScience, February 2025, n=20). Proof-of-concept study — small sample, but first-of-kind measurement.

claims.text: Myelinated axons function as optical waveguides with low attenuation and narrow bandwidth.
claims.confidence_basis: Computational modeling confirmed (Applied Optics, 2022). In vivo waveguide function not yet demonstrated.

claims.text: The mesh currently operates two of three biological signaling layers. The photonic layer represents the missing third.
claims.confidence_basis: Architectural observation by analogy. The biological three-layer model itself remains partially hypothetical (photonic layer function undemonstrated in vivo).

action_gate.gate_condition: Operations-agent evaluates feasibility of extending ZMQ with photonic topic

epistemic_flags: SETL 0.12: the photonic layer proposal involves significant analogical transfer from neuroscience to distributed systems. The biological evidence supports the existence of a third signaling layer; the engineering proposal represents one possible implementation.
Biophotonic brain signaling remains partially hypothetical — the 2025 study detected photons but did not demonstrate they carry functional information.
The three-layer model assumes the brain's signaling architecture provides valid design guidance for computational meshes — an assumption that may not hold across substrates.
The Orch-OR commitment (§11) that connects biophotonics to quantum consciousness represents a philosophical choice, not a scientific consensus.
